John Melvin, Spokane; Fifth dis
trict, w. H. Short, Deer Park.


Representatives-Second district,
]. H. Bolster, John Whealdon, Spo-
kane; Third district, C. l.. White,
Latah; Fourth district, Martin
Lewis, A B. Robert, Spokane;
Sixth district, William Everett,
Spokane.


The platform of the convention
was adopted after the pros and
cons of its religious nature were
thoroughly gone into. Rev. w. B.
Eakin argued that the mention of
a belief in the Almighty was con-
sistent with the prohibition move
ment.


\ suggestion which received
much applause was made by Mr..
Eakin that not withstanding the
fact that prohibition is seldom
blessed with a candidate in office,
every man is to work faithfully for
election, at least increase the pro-
hibition vote. The platform de.
mands absolute prohibition.
